algae_description: White/pale fuzzy growth coating a dark vertical structure (likely a sponge filter or filter column) — possibly bacterial biofilm or fungal fuzz rather than true algae, given the white coloration. Consistent with cycling tank biofilm.
concerns: White fuzzy coating on filter/hardscape — likely bacterial biofilm or fungal growth consistent with an active nitrogen cycle, but worth monitoring. If it persists or spreads post-cycle, may warrant a closer look.
Image is underexposed and taken from a side/back angle, limiting full tank assessment.
narrative: A close-up shot of what appears to be a sponge filter or filter column running vertically through the tank, heavily coated in white fuzzy biofilm — classic nitrogen cycle funk. One shrimp is visible mid-frame, small and pale, appearing to graze or explore the biofilm-covered surface, which is exactly the kind of foraging behavior you'd hope to see. The backlighting blows out the water column, but what's visible of the water itself looks clear.

substrate_condition: clean, light-colored substrate visible, no obvious debris accumulation

narrative: A sideways, slightly blurry interior shot of Media Luna revealing what appears to be a hang-on-back or internal filter mounted on the right wall, a heater, and a teal/blue object in the foreground — possibly an airline tube or decorative piece. Dense green plant growth is visible in the background, suggesting the tank is well-planted and progressing through the cycle with vegetation in place.

What appears to be a heater or thermometer probe is visible mid-frame along with what looks like filter intake equipment on the right side, and a bright teal/cyan object — possibly a thermometer or clip — is prominent in the lower-left of the frame. algae_description: Dense green/yellow-green growth coating the rear wall and possibly the back glass — consistent with green algae or biofilm establishing on surfaces. Could also be a moss or plant wall, but the coverage and texture suggest algae colonization.
substrate_condition: Light-colored, sandy or fine substrate visible along the tank bottom. Appears relatively clean with no obvious debris accumulation.
concerns: Image is rotated/sideways making full assessment difficult
Dense green/yellow-green growth on rear wall — could be algae bloom worth monitoring as cycle completes
Low image resolution and dark exposure limit detailed inspection
narrative: The tank interior is visible in this rotated, underexposed shot — equipment is prominent including what appears to be a hang-on-back filter and a teal/cyan object (possibly a thermometer or decorative item) in the midground. The rear wall is blanketed in dense green-yellow growth, which on Day 38 of a nitrogen cycle is unsurprising — algae tends to colonize hard surfaces as nutrients build up before the cycle stabilizes.

algae_description: Green growth visible along the back wall and substrate — likely green algae or early biofilm establishing on surfaces, consistent with a cycling tank on day 38.

narrative: The tank is rotated in frame and somewhat underexposed, but a single small red shrimp is visible in the midground, resting near the substrate. Equipment is visible in the upper right — what appears to be a hang-on-back or internal filter and a teal/cyan object that may be a heater or thermometer tube. Green algae growth coats the back wall, which is entirely normal and even encouraging on day 38 of a nitrogen cycle — the biofilm is developing nicely.
